title: Add M5 content schema and data-mod validation gate
summary: Implement the first M5 content validation gate and schema policy for anomaly roster, faction/governance hooks, season events and alpha catalog definitions without granting runtime code-mod authority.
acceptance: M5 content definitions for anomaly roster faction/governance hooks season events and alpha catalog entries have versioned schema validation and deterministic compile order
Data-mod inputs are validated for manifest shape path safety file size schema references semantics localization coverage and unsupported capabilities before runtime
Code mods network mods platform API mods executable scripts remote URLs unsafe paths recursive archive expansion and oversized assets fail closed with structured reasons
Compiled output records stable DefIndex ordering content manifest hash validation counters and failure diagnostics suitable for save/replay and Worker basis checks
Focused tests cover valid content invalid schema unknown references semantic failures missing localization unsafe paths unsupported capabilities and deterministic compile order

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.
Implemented M5 data-only content schema/data-mod validation gate with deterministic compiler output, focused tests, CLI mode, docs and report; all required checks pass.

changes_requested: High: manifest-declared size limits are parsed but not enforced against the content pack. validateM5ContentPack checks file and total bytes using only option/default caps before parseManifest returns manifest.maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes; parseManifest validates the declared caps are within defaults but does not compare them to actual file byte lengths or total byteCount. A probe pack declaring maxFileBytes=1 and maxTotalBytes=10 with 2673 bytes validated ok:true. This violates WM-0073 acceptance for manifest/file-size validation and oversized inputs failing closed. Enforce manifest caps after manifest parse or restructure validation so declared caps participate in file/total size diagnostics, and add focused tests for manifest maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes failures.
Repaired manifest-declared maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes enforcement against actual M5 pack bytes; added focused regressions; all WM-0073 required checks pass.

changes_requested: High: previous manifest-declared maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes repair is present for honest byte measurements, but validateM5ContentPack still trusts caller-provided M5ContentPackFile.byteLength instead of deriving or cross-checking bytes from file.text. A semantic-valid probe with manifest maxFileBytes=1 and maxTotalBytes=10, actual text total 4856 bytes, and every file byteLength spoofed to 1 returned ok:true with counters.byteCount=7 and no diagnostics. This violates WM-0073 file-size/oversized-input fail-closed acceptance for the exported validation entry point. Compute actual bytes from text for validation or reject mismatched byteLength metadata, then add a regression for spoofed byteLength under manifest-declared caps.
Repaired exported validator byte measurement to derive size/counters from file.text instead of caller byteLength metadata; added spoofed byteLength regression; all WM-0073 required checks pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified validateM5ContentPack now derives file and total byte counts from file.text, so spoofed M5ContentPackFile.byteLength cannot bypass manifest maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes; counters.byteCount reports actual UTF-8 text bytes. Original manifest cap enforcement, deterministic compiler evidence, fail-closed validation tests, allowed scope, and required gates are confirmed.

Reviewed M5 content validation gate accepted after manifest cap and spoofed byteLength repairs; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, focused m5-content-validation tests, git diff --check and pnpm quality. No runtime/protocol/save/package/M6 changes.

objective: Create the M5 data-only content validation gate before runtime M5 systems consume alpha content.
forbiddenPaths: code mods network mods platform API mods executable scripts
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: schema work becomes runtime behavior; ownership is limited to content-schema, content-compiler and content-cli validation surfaces.
Risk: unsafe mod inputs reach runtime; every accepted input must have a structured validation report and fail-closed tests.
Risk: public schema changes sprawl; ADR-0010 must be cited and any dependency/package-manifest need blocks.
benchmarkImpact: Adds validation counters and compile-order evidence only; no runtime benchmark baseline update until WM-0083.
reviewRouting: Systems-architect completes to reviewer; simulation-engineer must be consulted before runtime owner stores consume compiled M5 defs.
rollbackModel: Revert M5 schema/compiler/CLI validation changes, tests, docs and report; leave M4 content validation behavior intact.
ownerGate: Reject unsafe paths unsupported capabilities and executable content before runtime
Record stable content manifest and DefIndex evidence
Block on package manifest dependency public protocol save schema or M6 needs

sparkEligibility.reason: Content schema and data-mod validation define public content boundaries and security policy, so they require systems-architect ownership.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified validateM5ContentPack now derives file and total byte counts from file.text, so spoofed M5ContentPackFile.byteLength cannot bypass manifest maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes; counters.byteCount reports actual UTF-8 text bytes. Original manifest cap enforcement, deterministic compiler evidence, fail-closed validation tests, allowed scope, and required gates are confirmed.

integration.summary: Reviewed M5 content validation gate accepted after manifest cap and spoofed byteLength repairs; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, focused m5-content-validation tests, git diff --check and pnpm quality. No runtime/protocol/save/package/M6 changes.
